steps.title: Understand standard deviation and identify the key difference
steps.content: Both groups surveyed 2,500 men and reported the same mean height, 186 cm. That means the center of the two distributions is the same. The useful shortcut is to ignore the matching mean and compare only the standard deviations, because standard deviation measures spread around the mean.

steps.title: Use Desmos as a table check
steps.content: Enter the two standard deviations as a quick table/check: Group A has $12.5$ and Group B has $19.1$. Since $19.1>12.5$, Group B has the larger spread. The table does not give the tallest participant or either median, so choices B and D cannot be known from the information given.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C says the heights in Group B had a larger spread than the heights in Group A. That is exactly what the larger standard deviation shows, so choice C is correct.
